items.title: Tens of thousands celebrate with Mexico’s president after US delays tariffs
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Tens of thousands of people packed into Mexico’s City main plaza on Sunday to show support for President Claudia Sheinbaum as the U.S. decided to postpone tariffs on many of the country’s goods. (AP video by Amaranta Marentes)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: WATCH: Celebrations in India after cricket team wins Champions Trophy
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Celebrations erupted in Ahmedabad, Mumbai, Chennai and Guwahati after India won another global cricket tournament with a four-wicket victory over New Zealand in the Champions Trophy final on Sunday. (AP video by Ajit Solanki, Anupam Nath, Mahesh Kumar A, Rafiq Maqbool, Rajanish Kakade) Read more here: https://bit.ly/3FdPdQ6

#india #cricket #championstrophy2025 #news #sports</article>

items.title: One small business navigates Trump’s on-again, off-again tariffs on Canadian and Mexican goods
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The Trump's administration's on-again, off-again 25% tariffs on imports from Canada and Mexico — which went into effect on Tuesday only to be suspended on some items for a month on Thursday — are giving one small-business owner whiplash. (AP Video: Tassanee Vejpongsa)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Thousands held in detention on Myanmar border after being forced to scam others worldwide
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>In former scam compounds across southeastern Myanmar thousands of freed workers are stuck in limbo. Rescued from their previous lives as cyber scammers, many are now waiting to be repatriated. 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Panama releases dozens of migrants deported from the US
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Panama has released dozens of migrants deported from the United States who were held for weeks in a remote camp on the condition that they would leave the Central American nation in 30 days. It comes after weeks of lawsuits and human rights criticism. It thrust many into a legal limbo, scrambling to find a path forward. Read more here: https://bit.ly/4hiV7fW

#panama #usa #news 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: International Women’s Day: Thousands march for equality and justice across Latin America
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Thousands of women took to the streets of capitals across Latin America on Saturday to mark International Women's Day and demand gender equality and an end to gender-based violence. (AP Video by Sebastian Moscoso, Cesar Olmos and Cristian Kovadlof) Read more here: https://bit.ly/41RnCgo

#womensday #news #internationalwomensday

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Watch live view of cherry blossoms in Japan’s Kawazu as blooming season approaches.

items.title: Family narrowly avoids being struck by falling tree in New York
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A family narrowly missed being struck by a large tree in the Bensonhurst area of Brooklyn, New York. Video from a camera mounted at a neighbor's house shows the fall and the family's shocked reaction afterward.

#newyork #news #usa

items.title: AP reporter describes South Carolina's firing squad execution after witnessing it
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Reporter Jeffrey Collins witnessed a South Carolina man convicted of murder executed by firing squad Friday, the first U.S. prisoner to die by that method in 15 years. (AP video by Erik Verduzco) Read more here: https://bit.ly/3Fs3c4I

#southcarolina #news

items.title: Citizens rally support for Ukraine in Washington, DC, as US relations sour
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A crowd gathered at the Ellipse in Washington D.C. to protest the Trump Administration's recent policy of distancing support from Ukraine. U.S. and Ukraine officials will meet in Saudi Arabia next week to discuss ending the war with Russia.

#usa #ukraine #news

items.title: Man who scaled London's Big Ben clock tower with Palestinian flag comes down
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A man who brought central London to a halt after climbing one of the capital's landmark buildings has now returned to the ground.  The barefoot man, who appeared to be staging a protest on a ledge several meters (yards) up Elizabeth Tower, which houses Big Ben, stepped off the iconic building and onto a cherry picker after a long conversation with negotiators. Read more here: https://bit.ly/3Ffkd28

#london #news #bigben 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Funeral held in Idlib province for members of security forces killed in Syria clashes
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A funeral was held in Syria's Idlib province on Saturday to mourn four security members killed in clashes with loyalists of ousted President Bashar Assad, as a war monitor group said the death toll from two days of clashes and revenge killings had risen to more than 1,000. (AP video shot by Omar Albam) 


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Maasai girls take up self-defense as protection from sexual abuse and early marriage
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Maasai girls are taking up martial arts to protect themselves from sexual abuse and violence stemming from early marriage. The training at a boarding school in Transmara in western Kenya is an incentive for parents to allow their daughters to stay in school instead of undergoing the female genital cutting ritual that is still common in Maasai girls between the ages of 8 and 17. (AP video: Zelipha Kirobi/ Production: Jackson Njehia) Read more here: https://bit.ly/3FfAzba

#kenya #women #news 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: 12 people injured by 3 men shooting randomly at Toronto pub customers
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A dozen people were injured in a shooting at an eastern Toronto pub in what the police chief called "an incredibly brazen act of violence" by three men who entered the bar and fired randomly without warning. Read more here: https://bit.ly/4iALnyH

#news 

Picture credit: Arlyn McAdorey/The Canadian Press via AP
